title: T-022 — Register cost_method_selector_v1 + waste_cost_allocator_v1 + standard_cost_approval_v1 DSL rules (§10.1-10.4, 02-SETTINGS §7 delta)
prompt:
# T-022 — Register 10-FIN DSL rules in 02-SETTINGS rules_registry

PRD: docs/prd/10-FINANCE-PRD.md §10.1 cost_method_selector_v1; §10.2 waste_cost_allocator_v1; §10.3 standard_cost_approval_v1 (P1 stub); §10.4 registry update to 02-SETTINGS §7 delta (rules 13/14/15 of 15 total).
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Migration that INSERTs 3 rules into the 02-SETTINGS `rules_registry` table (foundation T-018/T-035 DSL registry). Each rule has its YAML definition stored as JSONB. Rule definitions match PRD §10.1, §10.2, §10.3 verbatim. P2 placeholder for standard_cost_approval_v1 stub (v2.0 dual sign-off deferred).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0059_register_finance_dsl_rules.sql` containing INSERTs into `rules_registry` (table schema per foundation T-018):
   - `cost_method_selector_v1` — type='conditional', entity='inventory_cost_layers|material_consumption_costs', version='1.0', triggers=['lp.received','material.consumed'], evaluation=JSONB matching §10.1 YAML lines 850-875, fallback_v0='wac', owner_module='10-FIN', status='active'.
   - `waste_cost_allocator_v1` — type='conditional', entity='wo_waste_log → work_order_costs.waste_cost_actual', version='1.0', triggers=['waste.logged'], evaluation=JSONB matching §10.2 lines 884-910, fallback_v0='full_loss', status='active'.
   - `standard_cost_approval_v1` — type='gate + workflow_as_data', entity='standard_costs', version='1.0', triggers=['standard_cost.submitted'], evaluation=JSONB matching §10.3 P1 simplified version (single_role_approve roles=[finance_manager, owner]), status='stub' (P2 evolution = v2.0 multi-level threshold rules).
   - All rules: org_id NULL (system rules) — they apply globally per multi-tenant pattern.
2. Rule evaluator hooks: `apps/web/app/finance/_lib/rules/evaluate.ts` exports `evaluateCostMethodSelector(orgId, event)` + `evaluateWasteCostAllocator(orgId, event)` server-side helpers. They read the rule JSONB from rules_registry at runtime (DSL = data-driven), apply the matching action, return outcome. Re-uses foundation rule-engine package if present.
3. ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE — idempotent: re-running migration updates definition_jsonb to latest version (allows DSL evolution without down-migration).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0059_register_finance_dsl_rules.sql [create]
- apps/web/app/finance/_lib/rules/evaluate.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/finance/_lib/rules/__tests__/evaluate.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
- Given migration 0059 ran, when SELECT rule_id FROM rules_registry WHERE owner_module='10-FIN', then 3 rows exist with the 3 rule_ids. AND given migration re-runs, when SELECT counts rows, then count is still 3 (idempotent ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).
- Given finance_settings.default_valuation_method='fifo' and lp.received event, when evaluateCostMethodSelector runs, then returns {action:'create_cost_layer', method:'fifo'}.
- Given default_valuation_method='wac' and material.consumed event, when evaluator runs, then returns {action:'consume_at_wac', method:'wac'}.
- Given waste.logged event with category.cost_allocation_method='full_loss', when evaluateWasteCostAllocator runs, then returns {waste_cost: qty*unit_cost, strategy:'full_loss'}.
_(AC count consolidated from 6→4 by Fixer F13 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost))_

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: evaluate.test.ts seeds rules + finance_settings + waste_categories fixtures; exercises FIFO and WAC paths + waste full_loss + standard_cost approval stub.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/finance/_lib/rules/__tests__/evaluate.test.ts`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not hardcode rule logic inside application code — DSL JSONB is the SoT; evaluator interprets it.
- Do not modify rules_registry rows owned by other modules.
- Do not omit fallback_v0 — rules must have a safe default.
- Do not skip ON CONFLICT — re-running migration must be safe.
- Do not introduce P2 logic for standard_cost_approval_v1 — P1 is single_role_approve stub only.
